chapter_summary: On the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary come to the tomb. A brilliant angel rolls away the stone and announces that Jesus is risen! The women run to tell the disciples, and Jesus meets them on the way. The religious leaders bribe the guards to spread a lie. Jesus appears to His disciples on a mountain in Galilee and gives them the Great Commission — to go and make disciples of all nations, baptising them and teaching them, with the promise that He is with them always.
main_lesson: Jesus rose from the dead! Nothing could keep Him in the tomb. And now He sends us out to tell the whole world the best news ever: Jesus is alive!

memory_verse.kjv: He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.
memory_verse.little_bible: The angel said: 'He is not here — He has risen, just as He said! Come and see the place where He was lying.'

parent_guide: Matthew 28 is the most joy-filled chapter in the Gospel. It answers the anguish of chapter 27 with triumphant certainty: death could not hold Jesus. Key moments to explore with children: the angel's declaration (v6) — emphasise that the resurrection is a historical fact, not just a feeling; Jesus meeting the women (vv9-10) — His first resurrection appearance is to women, which is deeply significant; the guards bribed (vv11-15) — a good moment to discuss what happens when people try to suppress the truth; the Great Commission (vv18-20) — every child is included in this mission. The final promise, 'I am with you always,' is the perfect note to end on: Jesus is not gone — He is present with us every day.
application_for_children: Jesus is alive right now — today! That means He hears your prayers, He is with you at school, at home, when you are scared and when you are happy. And He has a job for you too: tell others about Him. You do not have to be grown up to share the best news in the world.

verses.kjv: In the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.
verses.little_bible: Early on the first day of the week, just as Sunday began to dawn, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary made their way to the tomb to visit it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Early Sunday morning, Mary and Mary went to visit Jesus' tomb.
verses.meaning: These women came in grief, expecting to find death. Instead they were about to receive the greatest surprise in history. God often meets us when we are grieving and turns our tears to joy.
verses.memory_phrase: God turns our tears to joy.
verses.prayer: God, when I am sad, help me remember that You can turn everything around.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been sad about something and then got a wonderful surprise?
verses.family_discussion: The women came expecting death and found life. When has God surprised your family with something wonderful during a hard time?
verses.do_it_today: If you or someone you know is sad today, bring them this message: Jesus turns sorrow to joy.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two women in robes walking along a garden path in the soft light of very early morning, the sky pale pink and gold at the horizon, a rocky hillside ahead of them. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.
verses.little_bible: Suddenly there was a great earthquake! An angel came down from heaven, rolled back the massive stone from the door of the tomb — and sat on top of it!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The ground shook! An angel came from heaven and rolled away the big stone and sat on it.
verses.meaning: The stone was not rolled away to let Jesus out — He had already risen. It was rolled away so people could look in and see that He was gone. The angel sat on it triumphantly.
verses.memory_phrase: The stone was rolled away so we could see in.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the empty tomb. It is proof that You are alive!
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the angel sat on the stone?
verses.family_discussion: The stone wasn't moved to let Jesus out — He had already gone through it in His resurrection body! The stone was moved for the witnesses. What does that tell us about how God works?
verses.do_it_today: Draw or paint an empty tomb with the stone rolled away. Celebrate what it means.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing angel in brilliant white seated triumphantly on a large round stone rolled away from a tomb entrance, the garden bathed in golden morning light, earthquake cracks in the ground. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:
verses.little_bible: The angel's face shone like lightning, and his clothes were dazzling white as freshly fallen snow.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel shone like lightning and was dressed in dazzling white.
verses.meaning: The angel's brightness reflected heaven's joy over the resurrection. The glory of God always breaks through when the greatest things happen.
verses.memory_phrase: Heaven's joy shone through that angel.
verses.prayer: God, fill me with Your joy today — let it shine from my face.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most dazzling or brilliant thing you have ever seen?
verses.family_discussion: The angel's appearance was overwhelming. Why do you think God sends such glorious messengers for such important moments?
verses.do_it_today: Let your face show joy today — be a messenger of good news to everyone you meet.
verses.illustration_prompt: A brilliant angel seated on a stone, face glowing with golden-white light, robes shimmering like snow, garden trees and flowers vivid around the figure. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.
verses.little_bible: The guards shook with terror at the sight of the angel and fell to the ground like dead men.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The guards were so scared they fell down like they were dead.
verses.meaning: The soldiers who were supposed to keep Jesus in the tomb were overcome with fear and could do nothing. Heaven's power is always greater than any earthly force.
verses.memory_phrase: Nothing is more powerful than God.
verses.prayer: God, when I face scary things, help me remember that Your power is always greater.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most surprising thing that has ever made you jump or feel scared?
verses.family_discussion: The soldiers were there to stop something from happening — but God's power was far beyond anything they could contain. How does this encourage you?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'The power that raised Jesus lives in me through the Holy Spirit.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Roman soldiers in full armour tumbling backwards on the ground outside an open tomb, their weapons scattered, their faces showing shock and terror. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.
verses.little_bible: The angel turned to the women and said, 'Do not be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel said to the women, 'Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus.'
verses.meaning: The angel's first words were 'Fear not' — the same words God speaks throughout the whole Bible. He knows who we are looking for, and He meets us with comfort.
verses.memory_phrase: God says: 'Do not be afraid!'
verses.prayer: God, thank You for telling me not to be afraid. Help me believe it.
verses.discussion_question: What is something that makes you afraid? Can you bring that to Jesus today?
verses.family_discussion: God says 'fear not' 365 times in the Bible — one for every day of the year. Which fears does your family need to bring to Him?
verses.do_it_today: Write 'Do not be afraid' on a piece of paper and put it somewhere you can see it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A brilliant angel speaking gently and tenderly to two women kneeling in awe in a sunlit garden, one hand raised in reassurance. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.
verses.little_bible: The angel said: 'He is not here — He has risen, just as He said! Come, look inside and see the place where He was lying.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel said: 'He is not here! He has risen! Come and see!'
verses.meaning: These are the most important words in history: He is risen! Jesus kept His promise. Death could not hold Him. Everything He said is true.
verses.memory_phrase: He is not here — He is risen!
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are alive! You kept Your promise! I celebrate You today.
verses.discussion_question: If you had heard these words yourself, what would you have felt?
verses.family_discussion: He rose 'as He said' — Jesus had predicted this and it came true exactly. What promises of Jesus is your family counting on for the future?
verses.do_it_today: Shout it out: 'Jesus is risen!' Tell as many people as you can today.
verses.illustration_prompt: An angel gesturing toward the open, empty tomb entrance, morning light flooding in, fresh white grave cloths lying on a stone shelf visible inside, the garden in full bloom outside. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.
verses.little_bible: The angel said, 'Go quickly! Tell His disciples that He is risen from the dead. He is going ahead of you to Galilee — you will see Him there. I have told you!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel said, 'Go quickly! Tell His disciples Jesus is risen! He will meet you in Galilee!'
verses.meaning: The first evangelists were these women. The first command after the resurrection was 'Go and tell.' The good news has always been meant to be shared.
verses.memory_phrase: Go and tell — Jesus is alive!
verses.prayer: Jesus, give me courage to go and tell others that You are alive!
verses.discussion_question: Who is the first person you would call with the best news ever?
verses.family_discussion: The women were commissioned to share the resurrection news before any of the apostles were. What does that say about who Jesus uses to spread His message?
verses.do_it_today: Tell at least one person today: 'Jesus is alive! That changes everything!'
verses.illustration_prompt: Two women turning from an open garden tomb in a burst of movement and joy, the angel behind them pointing toward the open road, morning light flooding the scene. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.
verses.little_bible: The women rushed away from the tomb, their hearts full of both awe and great joy, running as fast as they could to tell the disciples!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The women ran from the tomb, filled with joy. They ran to tell the disciples!
verses.meaning: Fear and great joy together — the right response to an encounter with the risen Jesus. They didn't debate it or wait — they ran.
verses.memory_phrase: When Jesus is real, we cannot keep it to ourselves.
verses.prayer: Jesus, fill me with so much joy about You that I just have to tell people.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever run to tell someone something wonderful? What was it?
verses.family_discussion: The women ran. Not walked. Not planned. Ran. What does that kind of urgency look like when we share the gospel with people around us?
verses.do_it_today: Run — literally — to someone and share something true and wonderful about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two women in robes running along a garden path in early morning light, their faces full of wonder and joy, hands slightly outstretched in excitement, the sky golden above. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.
verses.little_bible: While the women were on their way, Jesus Himself met them! He said, 'Greetings!' They fell at His feet and worshipped Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: As they ran, Jesus met them! They fell down and worshipped Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus ran toward the grieving even before they reached the disciples. He met them on the road of obedience. When we move in obedience, Jesus meets us.
verses.memory_phrase: When we move in obedience, Jesus meets us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me take steps of obedience today, trusting that You will meet me on the way.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to worship someone?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus appeared to the women first. These were people who had been faithful and present at the cross. How does faithfulness position us to encounter Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Worship Jesus right now — not just with words, but with your posture, your whole heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing in a sunlit garden path with His arms wide open, two women kneeling at His feet in wonder and worship, flowers and green trees surrounding the scene. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to them, 'Do not be afraid! Go tell my brothers to go to Galilee — they will see Me there.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Don't be afraid! Go tell them to go to Galilee to see Me.'
verses.meaning: Even after rising from the dead, Jesus' first concern is for His friends. He calls the disciples 'my brothers' — a word full of grace, even for those who had run away.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus still calls us His family.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for calling me Your brother and sister even when I fail. Your love amazes me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to know Jesus calls you His brother or sister?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples had abandoned Jesus, but He still called them 'my brothers.' What does that tell you about how Jesus treats people who fail Him?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today: 'Jesus calls you His family. He is not giving up on you.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking tenderly to two women who are kneeling before Him in a garden, His hands gently raised in reassurance, warm golden sunlight streaming through the trees. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.
verses.little_bible: While the women were on their way, some of the guards went back to the city and reported everything that had happened to the chief priests.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: While the women ran, some guards went to report to the leaders what happened.
verses.meaning: Even the guards who witnessed the resurrection ran to the wrong people with the news. The same event produced worship in the women and a cover-up in others. Truth divides.
verses.memory_phrase: The same truth can produce worship or rejection.
verses.prayer: God, help me always respond to truth about Jesus with worship, not excuses.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the guards went to the chief priests instead of believing in Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The guards saw the same miracle but ran to suppress it rather than worship. Why do people sometimes run away from the truth about Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to help you always be honest about what you know is true about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Roman soldiers walking urgently through city gates into Jerusalem in the early morning, their armour dusty, their expressions troubled, people on the streets turning to watch. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers,
verses.little_bible: The chief priests called a meeting with the elders. After talking it over, they counted out a large sum of money and paid the soldiers.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders met together and paid the soldiers a lot of money.
verses.meaning: Their response to the resurrection was: throw money at it. You cannot buy the truth away — but people keep trying.
verses.memory_phrase: Truth cannot be bought or buried.
verses.prayer: God, protect the truth about Jesus from all the lies people tell. Help it shine.
verses.discussion_question: If someone paid you to tell a lie, would you do it? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: The leaders tried to silence the resurrection with money. What does that tell us about how threatened they were by the truth?
verses.do_it_today: Be honest today — even when it costs you something.
verses.illustration_prompt: Religious leaders seated in a stone meeting room counting out silver coins across a table to waiting soldiers, their faces conspiratorial. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
verses.little_bible: They told the soldiers to say: 'His disciples came in the night and stole His body while we were asleep.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They told the soldiers to lie: 'Say the disciples stole the body while you slept.'
verses.meaning: Sleeping guards would face execution under Roman law. Yet the leaders bribed them to tell this impossible story. When the truth is inconvenient, some people choose lies.
verses.memory_phrase: Truth is always stronger than lies.
verses.prayer: God, help me always choose truth, even when a lie would feel easier.
verses.discussion_question: Why are lies dangerous, even when they seem to help in the short term?
verses.family_discussion: This lie has been repeated for thousands of years — yet the empty tomb remains unexplained. How does that make you more confident in the resurrection?
verses.do_it_today: Look up one piece of evidence for the resurrection and share it with someone.
verses.illustration_prompt: A soldier and a robed leader shaking hands over a table with coins, both looking over their shoulders, the room shadowed and secretive. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And if this come to the governor's ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.
verses.little_bible: They added, 'And if the governor hears about this, we will handle it and make sure you don't get in trouble.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders promised to protect the soldiers if the governor found out.
verses.meaning: They were building an entire structure of deception on top of the resurrection. But lies always require more lies to hold them up.
verses.memory_phrase: One lie always needs more lies.
verses.prayer: God, help me be a person of truth. Keep me from the trap of lies.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever told a small lie and then needed another lie to cover it? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: Notice how the leaders needed to promise more deception to maintain the first lie. How does choosing honesty keep our lives simpler and cleaner?
verses.do_it_today: Commit today to being completely honest — no small untruths, no half-truths.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of leaders and soldiers gathered close together in a shadowed courtyard, speaking in hushed voices, one leader placing a hand reassuringly on a soldier's shoulder. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.
verses.little_bible: The soldiers took the money and spread the false story — and this made-up explanation was still being passed around among people even when Matthew wrote his Gospel.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The soldiers took the money and told the lie. Some people believed it for years.
verses.meaning: Lies can travel far and last long. But the resurrection has outlasted every lie about it. Two thousand years later, Jesus is still worshipped and the tomb is still empty.
verses.memory_phrase: The truth of the resurrection outlasts every lie.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your resurrection is true and no lie can ever change that. I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a true story that has lasted for a very long time?
verses.family_discussion: People have been trying to explain away the resurrection for two thousand years. Why do you think none of those explanations have actually worked?
verses.do_it_today: Share one true thing about Jesus with someone who may have heard a different story.
verses.illustration_prompt: A split scene — in one half, a person whispering to another in a market; in the other half, a glowing empty tomb with an open door. Light versus shadow. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
verses.little_bible: The eleven disciples went to Galilee — to the mountain where Jesus had told them to meet Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to go.
verses.meaning: Obedience opened the door to an encounter with the risen Jesus. They went where He said to go — and He was there.
verses.memory_phrase: Obey what Jesus says, and you will find Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me obey Your word and go where You call me to go.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever followed instructions and been glad you did?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples obeyed the instructions to go to Galilee — and they met the risen Jesus. How does obedience to God's Word lead us toward Him?
verses.do_it_today: Find one instruction Jesus gives in the Gospels and do it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Eleven people walking along a mountain path in Galilee, the blue hills stretching out behind them, morning light on their faces, anticipation in their posture. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.
verses.little_bible: When they saw Jesus, they fell down and worshipped Him. But some of them still had doubts.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When they saw Jesus, they worshipped. But some of them still doubted.
verses.meaning: Even here — in the presence of the risen Jesus — some doubted. Jesus didn't send them away. He still gave them the Great Commission. Doubt does not disqualify us from God's mission.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus still uses us even when we have questions.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I have doubts, help me bring them to You. You are big enough for my questions.
verses.discussion_question: Is it okay to have questions or doubts about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples had doubts but Jesus still commissioned them. What do you think He wants us to do when we have doubts — run away or come closer?
verses.do_it_today: If you have a question about Jesus, write it down and start looking for the answer.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of disciples on a green hillside in Galilee, some kneeling in worship, some standing with expressions of awe and wonder, all facing the same direction. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.
verses.little_bible: Jesus came and spoke to them: 'All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'All authority in heaven and on earth belongs to Me.'
verses.meaning: The one who was mocked as a pretend king is now Lord of the entire universe. His commission comes from absolute authority. We go in the name of the most powerful King who has ever existed.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus has all authority — everywhere.
verses.prayer: King Jesus, all authority belongs to You. Help me live as someone who belongs to the King.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to have authority? Who is the most powerful person you can think of?
verses.family_discussion: Every other authority in the world is limited. Jesus' authority is total — heaven and earth. How does that change the way we pray? The way we obey?
verses.do_it_today: Pray about a big problem in your life, remembering that you are praying to the One who holds all authority.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on a Galilean hillside with arms slightly outstretched, the landscape stretching out to the horizon, the sky wide and bright above Him, His disciples gathered in awe. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:
verses.little_bible: He told them: 'Go and make disciples of all nations — baptising them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Go and make disciples of all nations, baptising them in God's name.'
verses.meaning: The Great Commission is not optional — it is for every believer, including children. Go. Teach. Baptise. The whole world needs to hear about Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Go and tell the whole world about Jesus!
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to be part of Your mission. Use me to tell others about You — starting today.
verses.discussion_question: How can a child share the good news about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The commission says 'all nations' — every people group, every language. How can your family be part of that mission, even from where you live?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person from a different background you can share Jesus with. Pray for them by name.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing outward toward a wide horizon where many different people from different lands are shown, rays of light spreading outward like a sunrise. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world. Amen.
verses.little_bible: He said: 'Teach them to obey everything I have commanded you. And remember — I am with you always, to the very end of the age.' Amen!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Teach them everything I taught you. And I am with you always!' Amen!
verses.meaning: The greatest promise Jesus ever made: I am with you always. Not sometimes. Not when you get it right. Always. This is the promise we go out into the world carrying.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is with me — always.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for promising to be with me always. I am never alone. Never.
verses.discussion_question: When is a time you need to remember that Jesus is with you?
verses.family_discussion: 'I am with you always' — at school, in hard conversations, in scary places, at night. Where does your family need to hear this promise most right now?
verses.do_it_today: Write 'Jesus is with me always' somewhere you will see it all day. Let it be your confidence.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus on a sunlit hillside with arms wide, His disciples spread out before Him with joy on their faces, the landscape below them bright and wide, the sky golden and full of light. Storybook watercolour style.
